arts.ttl: Arne Slot not satisfied despite record-setting start to life at Liverpool
arts.sum: The Dutch manager is the first manager to win eight of his first nine matches in charge of the Reds.

arts.ttl: Liverpool transfer news: Arne Slot lining up shock £42m bid for Champions League star?
arts.sum: Liverpool reportedly enter the race to sign a 22-year-old attacker who has starred in the Champions League this season.

arts.ttl: Arne Slot makes Liverpool history with feat even Jurgen Klopp couldn't manage - but still believes Reds can improve after easing past Bologna
arts.sum: LEWIS STEELE AT ANFIELD: Arne Slot became the first Liverpool manager in history to win eight of his first nine games, but says his team must improve if they are to kick on this season.

arts.ttl: Martin Zubimendi clears up rumours he regretted not joining Liverpool in summer
arts.sum: Real Sociedad star Martin Zubimendi clears up rumours suggesting that he regrets not joining Liverpool in the summer.

arts.ttl: Italiano: “We came away from Anfield with our heads held high”
arts.sum: Despite suffering a 2-0 defeat against Liverpool at Anfield, Bologna’s manager Vincenzo Italiano expressed pride in his team’s performance in their Champions League encounter. The Italian side showed courage and determination against one of Europe’s top clubs, creating several chances throughout the match. Italiano began his post-match comments by stating, “We leave Anfield as we wanted: with our heads held high. We played a great game. We could have done better only on the first goal, but we showed great personality and created opportunities. This match was prepared with little time available.” The Bologna coach highlighted the team’s brave approach, The post Italiano: “We came away from Anfield with our heads held high” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Player ratings: Sensational Salah lifts Liverpool again in Champions League
arts.sum: Liverpool are cruising in the UEFA Champions League, thanks to Mohamed Salah's team-best performance in a win over Bologna.

arts.ttl: Arne Slot singles out Liverpool star after admitting he couldn't be happy with record win
arts.sum: Liverpool beat Bologna 2-0 at Anfield thanks to goals from Alexis Mac Allister and Mohamed Salah, but boss Arne Slot said there were elements of the display he didn't like

arts.ttl: Arne Slot way fast becoming clear as Liverpool supporters make feelings known to UEFA
arts.sum: The Ian Doyle verdict on a record-breaking night for Arne Slot and his star man Mohamed Salah, who inspired Liverpool's 2-0 Champions League win over Bologna at Anfield

arts.ttl: Trent Alexander-Arnold contract talks revealed as Liverpool encounter 'problem'
arts.sum: As things stand, Trent Alexander-Arnold, Virgil van Dijk and Mohamed Salah are only tied down at Anfield for the next few months as Liverpool decide how to tackle their respective contract situations.

arts.ttl: Liverpool’s latest Champions League win was almost 20 months in the making - and one big change is key
arts.sum: Liverpool 2-0 Bologna: Mac Allister and Salah scored as the Reds made it two from two in Europe
